title: Checkbox Group Item
description: An array-family variable widget rendering a set of independent checkboxes, one per option, from which a viewer ticks zero or more values. It binds one document/container variable (the `variable` it sets) whose declared type is `array`; on change the ticked values flow through the resolver's override path as an array and re-resolve the document so dependent `${var}`/`$var` consumers update live. A checkbox-group may only bind a variable whose declared type is `array` — the resolver enforces this widget↔variable type compatibility and reports WIDGET_TYPE_MISMATCH otherwise. It is an option-constrained array widget: it requires a bounded `{value,label}` option set (the resolver reports RESOLVE_CONFIG_INVALID when the option set is absent), in contrast to the freeform `tag-input`. The variable itself is declared in the document/container `variables`, which supplies the effective default; this config declares the binding, its presentation, the selectable option set, and how the displayed options are ordered. A checkbox-group never has children.

constraints.description: The human label rendered beside the control — editable at runtime.
When true the control is rendered read-only — editable at runtime to gate the input.
How the option set is ordered for display — editable at runtime.

variable.description: Name of the variable this widget sets. Must be a variable visible in the widget's scope (declared at document scope or on an ancestor container) and declared with a type the widget's family permits (array family → array). The values ticked here become that variable's runtime override on re-resolve.

label.description: Optional label rendered above or beside the group.

description.description: Optional help text describing what the input controls, rendered beneath or beside the control.

disabled.description: When true the control is rendered read-only and cannot be changed.

options.description: Selectable options, in declared display order. Each option is a value the bound array may contain when ticked, plus an optional human label. Option-constrained array widgets require a bounded option set; an absent set is reported by the resolver as RESOLVE_CONFIG_INVALID. Shares the enum family's `{value,label}` shape.

value.description: A value the bound array may contain when this option is ticked.

label.description: Optional display label for this option; defaults to the value when omitted.

sort.description: Optional ordering applied to the displayed options. `declared` (the default) keeps the options in the order they are listed; `label` sorts ascending by display label (falling back to value); `value` sorts ascending by value.
